title: Artificial allosteric protein switches with machine-learning-designed receptors

primary_findings.key_breakthrough: Machine-learning-engineered ligand-binding domains can function as efficient biosensor receptors without global conformational change
primary_findings.mechanism: Ligand binding reduces conformational entropy of system, increasing reporter domain activity
primary_findings.significance: Challenges paradigm that allosteric regulation requires global conformational change

conformational_entropy_coupling.finding: Fast internal protein dynamics (conformational entropy) can regulate allosteric function
conformational_entropy_coupling.implication: Expands repertoire of domains suitable for allosteric switch construction
conformational_entropy_coupling.advantage: Accessible pool of natural domains significantly increased

switch_characteristics: Colorimetric, luminescent, and electrochemical biosensors successfully engineered
Multi-input logic gates (YES and AND gates) successfully constructed
Both single-component and fully synthetic configurations achieved

beta_lactamase_chimeras.achievement: Demonstrated activity without global conformation change

biosensor_design_outcomes.design_principle: Linker positioning opposite ligand-binding cavity and away from flexible loops

hydrogen_deuterium_exchange_mass_spectro.finding: Revealed conformational entropy effects of ligand binding
hydrogen_deuterium_exchange_mass_spectro.insight: Supports ligand binding reduces system conformational entropy

nuclear_magnetic_resonance.finding: Confirmed conformational entropy as allosteric mediator

cellular_engineering.demonstration: E. coli cells with steroid-dependent antibiotic resistance

synthetic_biology_impact.practical_utility: Demonstrated with functional engineered cells and bioelectronic devices
synthetic_biology_impact.diversity_achieved: Varied ligand-binding domain origins and specificities

information_processing_capability.fundamental_insight: Protein allostery underlies most biological information and energy processing
information_processing_capability.achievement: Successfully engineered artificial protein networks with programmable inputs and outputs
information_processing_capability.future_potential: Foundation for synthetic biological systems with complex information processing
